perturbation.original_text: Each Party also agrees during the Standstill Period not to request the other Party (or its directors, officers, employees or agents), directly or indirectly, to amend or waive any provision of this Section 8 (including this sentence); provided, that this provision shall not prohibit any request to amend or waive any provision of this Section 8 which is not publicly announced or disclosed by the requesting party and does not otherwise violate this Section 8.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party also agrees during the Standstill Period not to request the other Party (or its directors, officers, employees or agents), directly or indirectly, to amend or waive any provision of this Section 8 (including this sentence).
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'provided' clause, all requests to amend or waive any provision are prohibited. This creates an in-text contradiction as it removes the exception for non-publicly disclosed requests. If a party privately requests an amendment and the other party agrees, it is unclear if this is a breach, given the omission. It has an uncertainty for enforcement. The standstill is in contradiction with any confidential requests for an amendement
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party also agrees during the Standstill Period not to request the other Party (or its directors, officers, employees or agents), directly or indirectly, to amend or waive any provision of this Section 8 (including this sentence).
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for requests to amend or waive provisions of Section 8 if they were not publicly announced. The changed text removes this exception, creating a blanket prohibition on any requests. Since the Contradicted Text is the original text, it establishes an implicit agreement that no requests can happen. This is a contradiction with the changed text, because that exception has been removed, thus creating a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Company understands and agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by either Company or any of its Representatives and that the Company against which such breach is committed shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach or threat thereof. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by either Company of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the Company against which such breach is committed.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Company understands and agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by either Company or any of its Representatives and that the Company against which such breach is committed shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach or threat thereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by either Company of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the Company against which such breach is committed', the agreement now implies that the listed equitable remedies (injunction and specific performance) are the only recourse for breaches. This omission contradicts the general principle of contract law, making enforcement uncertain because it suggests that other legal or equitable remedies are waived. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because it creates uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Company understands and agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by either Company or any of its Representatives and that the Company against which such breach is committed shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach or threat thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the phrase 'Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by either Company of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the Company against which such breach is committed'. This omission implies that equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, are the exclusive remedies, which contradicts the original intent and creates uncertainty in enforcement, as the contradicted text (which is the same as the original text before modification, which is located elsewhere in the agreement) does not state that the equitable relief are the exclusive remedies.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the Companies regarding the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements, understandings, arrangements and. discussions between the Companies regarding such subject matter.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the Companies regarding the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements, understandings, arrangements.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'discussions between the Companies regarding such subject matter', the contract implies that prior discussions may still be relevant. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because, while the contract aims to be the 'entire agreement', the exclusion of prior discussions introduces ambiguity, potentially allowing parties to argue that those discussions should be considered in interpreting the agreement, creating a conflict. Now it creates a contradiction on what has to be taken into consideration to enforce this Agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the Companies regarding the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements, understandings, arrangements.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'discussions between the Companies regarding such subject matter' from the clause stating that the agreement contains the entire understanding. This omission creates ambiguity, potentially allowing parties to argue that prior discussions should be considered in interpreting the agreement. This contradicts the stated intention of the contradicted text, which is identical to the original text, which aims to be the 'entire agreement', thus creating an in-text contradiction.
